# services.openvpn

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `services.openvpn`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `services.openvpn.package` | `package` | The openvpn package to use. |
| `services.openvpn.restartAfterSleep` | `boolean` | Whether OpenVPN client should be restarted after sleep. |
| `services.openvpn.servers` | `attribute set of (submodule)` | Each attribute of this option defines a systemd service that runs an OpenVPN instance. These can be OpenVPN servers or clients. The name of each systemd service is `openvpn-«name».service`, where «name» is the corresponding attribute name. |
| `services.openvpn.servers.<name>.authUserPass` | `null or (optionally newline-terminated) single-line string or (submodule)` | This option can be used to store the username / password credentials with the "auth-user-pass" authentication method. You can either provide an attribute set of `username` and `password`, or the path to a file containing the credentials on two lines. WARNING: If you use an attribute set, this option will put the credentials WORLD-READABLE into the Nix store! |
| `services.openvpn.servers.<name>.autoStart` | `boolean` | Whether this OpenVPN instance should be started automatically. |
| `services.openvpn.servers.<name>.config` | `strings concatenated with "\n"` | Configuration of this OpenVPN instance. See {manpage}`openvpn(8)` for details. To import an external config file, use the following definition: `config = "config /path/to/config.ovpn"` |
| `services.openvpn.servers.<name>.down` | `strings concatenated with "\n"` | Shell commands executed when the instance is shutting down. |
| `services.openvpn.servers.<name>.up` | `strings concatenated with "\n"` | Shell commands executed when the instance is starting. |
| `services.openvpn.servers.<name>.updateResolvConf` | `boolean` | Use the script from the update-resolv-conf package to automatically update resolv.conf with the DNS information provided by openvpn. The script will be run after the "up" commands and before the "down" commands. |
